# checks.base.BaseCheck { #pycmdcheck.checks.base.BaseCheck }

```python
checks.base.BaseCheck()
```

Abstract base class for implementing checks.

This is the recommended way to implement custom checks. Subclasses
must override the `name`, `description` attributes and implement
the `run` method.

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Unique identifier for the check. Override in subclass.

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description. Override in subclass.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Create a custom check:

```python
>>> from pycmdcheck.checks.base import BaseCheck
>>> from pycmdcheck.results import CheckResult, CheckStatus
>>>
>>> class CopyrightCheck(BaseCheck):
...     name = "copyright"
...     description = "Check for copyright headers"
...
...     def run(self, package_path, config):
...         # Check logic here
...         return CheckResult(
...             name=self.name,
...             status=CheckStatus.OK,
...             message="All files have copyright headers",
...         )
```

Register via entry points in pyproject.toml:

```toml
[project.entry-points."pycmdcheck.checks"]
copyright = "my_package.checks:CopyrightCheck"
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.base.BaseCheck.run) | Execute the check and return result. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.base.BaseCheck.run }

```python
checks.base.BaseCheck.run(package_path, config)
```

Execute the check and return result.

Subclasses must implement this method to perform the actual check.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory to check. This is always an absolute, resolved path.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary for this check. Contains any options specified in pyproject.toml under [tool.pycmdcheck.checks.<name>]. Common keys include "enabled" (bool) and check-specific options.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult with:

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - name: Should match self.name

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - status: One of CheckStatus values (OK, WARNING, ERROR, etc.)

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - message: Brief description of the result

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   - details: Optional list of specific issues found

#### Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> def run(self, package_path, config):
...     issues = self._find_issues(package_path)
...     if issues:
...         return CheckResult(
...             name=self.name,
...             status=CheckStatus.WARNING,
...             message=f"Found {len(issues)} issues",
...             details=issues,
...         )
...     return CheckResult(
...         name=self.name,
...         status=CheckStatus.OK,
...         message="No issues found",
...     )
```
